design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate grid-based maze levels, fight enemies, manage ammunition and health, reach exit elevator or defeat boss
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (ammo/health) vs enemy encounters; exploration for secrets vs speed
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Allied spy conducting covert WW2 operations against Nazi forces; tactical infiltration and combat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Fast-paced violent action prioritized over stealth (reimagining Castle Wolfenstein); technical 3D perspective at fixed height; grid-based level design constrains spatial freedom but enables predictable navigation

difficulty.lives_system: Player begins each episode with 4 lives; lose one life when health reaches zero, restart current level with knife, pistol, and 8 bullets; extra lives earned through high scores or special tokens
difficulty.checkpointing: Original version allows save at any point; many ports restrict saves to between levels only
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Low mechanical agency; no difficulty settings documented; player can optimize for secrets and treasures to gain extra lives as buffer

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon acquisition through level placement: knife → pistol → submachine gun → chain gun; health and ammo management
progression.unlock_mechanism: Key collection gates access to locked doors; episodes gate access to later content
replayability_hooks: Secret room discovery for treasure and supplies
Point scoring system tracks completion ratio, speed, enemy kills, treasure collection, par time

world_building_techniques: Level theming around Nazi bunkers and military installations
Enemy types (guards, soldiers, dogs) reinforce WW2 setting
Episode naming ('Escape from Castle Wolfenstein', 'Operation: Eisenfaust', 'Die, Führer, Die!') provides loose narrative framing

boss_design.philosophy: Episode finale encounters; significantly more difficult than standard enemies
notable_bosses: Final boss fights conclude each episode
Described as 'particularly difficult enemy' encounters requiring sustained engagement

danger_signals: Nazi imagery and symbolism throughout bunker environments
environmental_storytelling: Bunker/castle architecture establishes military compound setting
Treasure placement suggests looting wartime installations

sources_used: wikipedia - Wolfenstein 3D article (Gameplay, Plot sections; Spear of Destiny overview)
